phase1.output: (Full To-Do List application with add, complete, delete, and stats functionality)
phase1.explanation:
This lesson combines everything you've learned:

To-Do List App features:
• Add tasks with due dates
• Mark tasks as complete
• Delete tasks
• Clear completed tasks
• Statistics display
• Custom cell rendering

Key concepts used:
• ObservableList with data binding
• ListView with custom cell factory
• Properties and binding
• Event handling
• Dialog/Alert integration
• Layouts (VBox, HBox)

Calculator App features:
• Basic arithmetic operations
• Clear function
• Custom grid layout

Design patterns:
• Model-View-Controller (MVC)
• Observable data
• Event-driven programming

Best practices:
• Separate data model (Task)
• UI updates via binding
• Input validation
• User feedback (status label)

What to add next:
• Save/load tasks (file I/O)
• Edit tasks
• Categories/tags
• Search/filter
• Sorting

This mini-app combines all JavaFX concepts into a working application!

phase2.errorMessage: (Tasks are added to the data but the UI DOESN'T UPDATE!)
phase2.explanation:
You added a task to the ObservableList but didn't refresh the ListView.

FIX: Update the ListView:
listView.setItems(tasks);

Or better: Use binding/listener so UI updates automatically.

Option 1: Bind listView.itemsProperty().bind(tasks);
Option 2: Set items once and ObservableList updates UI automatically

In our To-Do app, we call updateTaskListView() after every change.

Always ensure UI updates when data changes!

phase3.prompt:
Create a simple Contact Manager app with:
1. A TableView with columns: Name, Phone, Email
2. TextFields for adding new contacts
3. An "Add Contact" button that adds to the table
4. A "Delete Contact" button that removes the selected contact
5. Show the total number of contacts

Use ObservableList for data and PropertyValueFactory for columns.
dialogueHints: Contact model with name, phone, email properties
phase3.openingDialogue: Mini App — bring it all together! 🚀
